contracts.name: labor-domain-relationship
contracts.pass: One normative ontology covers organization, account, human principal, employee principal, assignment, access, authority, custody, payer, beneficiary, supervisor, lifecycle, provenance, and restricted launch policy.
contracts.fail: Any overloaded identifier independently represents conflicting authority, custody, or accounting semantics.

contracts.name: authorization-decision
contracts.pass: One evaluator consumes actor, context, resource, action, current relationship/grant, policy version, and environment and is used by RLS and all enabled routes.
contracts.fail: Bearer possession, account membership, phone ownership, caller headers, or mutable request IDs are sufficient authority.

contracts.name: durable-command-effect
contracts.pass: Stable intent, immutable command, atomic claim, durable attempt, accepted|failed|ambiguous receipt, reconciliation, and deterministic replay are implemented.
contracts.fail: Read-then-write owns a race, replay repeats an effect, or success is returned without a durable receipt.

contracts.name: consequential-protocol-envelope
contracts.pass: Unknown major versions reject and supported old versions deterministically upcast while preserving original payload/hash.
contracts.fail: Replayed work can silently acquire new semantics after deployment.

contracts.name: provider-capability-receipt
contracts.pass: Each provider operation is classified as native_idempotency, queryable_receipt, consumer_dedupe, or non_idempotent_ambiguous and retry follows that class.
contracts.fail: A universal exactly-once assumption or blind retry can duplicate an irreversible effect.

contracts.name: release-state-proof
contracts.pass: Proof manifests bind SHA, environment, timestamp, script version, digest, redaction state, and evidence IDs; release states remain separate.
contracts.fail: Docs, fixtures, estimator results, confidence, or narrow CI promote deployment/provider/commercial/live state.

findings.validation: Behavioral relationship matrix plus migration/backfill and real Supabase acceptance.
findings.fail: Employee identity, assignment, custody, payer, beneficiary, or authority still collapses into account ownership.

findings.validation: Real Supabase RLS and route matrix across accounts, users, employees, roles, SMS/channel, artifacts, and revocation.
findings.fail: Any path authorizes solely on account membership or employees.account_id.

findings.validation: Principal-bound immutable approval and credential denial matrix with browser/SMS/provider packet.
findings.fail: Bearer possession or mutable caller data determines approval authority or effect content.

findings.validation: 100-way claim race, double tap, lost response, crash before/after provider acceptance, contradictory outcome test.
findings.fail: Concurrent/replayed approval can produce two terminal effects or no durable outcome.

findings.validation: Wrong audience/capability, leaked token, replay, expiry, caller ID spoof, owner actor spoof.
findings.fail: A global Manager credential can invoke arbitrary tools across assignments.

findings.validation: Session theft, rotation, role downgrade, relationship revocation, wrong purpose/principal/assignment.
findings.fail: A long-lived session/link remains authoritative after current relationship or role changes.

findings.validation: Duplicate submit, simultaneous claim, Auth timeout, split-brain failure after every step, retry and repair replay.
findings.fail: Retry duplicates identity or partial success requires manual database surgery.

findings.validation: Parallel budget race, restart, replicas, audit outage, provider timeout before/after acceptance, duplicate request, reconciliation.
findings.threshold: 0 beyond declared tolerance; 0 success without receipt
findings.fail: Process-local counters or missing audit persistence permit untracked spend.

findings.validation: Primer DB timeout, duplicate primer, session rotation, worker crash, downstream timeout.
findings.fail: Claim failure still mutates context or silently advances state.

findings.validation: Double submit, lost HTTP response, browser reconnect, queued older turn, deterministic replay.
findings.fail: One human intent creates duplicate turns/effects or replay returns a different result.

findings.validation: Two assignments per employee/provider, wrong Twilio sender, moved/revoked binding, duplicate/out-of-order/replay/ambiguous event, real provider packets.
findings.fail: Provider subject or connector custody falls back to account-wide ownership.

findings.validation: Blank DB, snapshot clone, rerun, lock contention, partial migration, grant drift, rollback rehearsal, real staging/production Supabase verifier.
findings.fail: Production schema and executable source are different release states.

findings.validation: Seed web compile, lint, RLS, race, protocol, and missing-artifact failures and verify required checks block.
findings.fail: Green status represents only a narrow backend slice.

findings.validation: Proof manifest schema, SHA/digest binding, real browser/provider/runtime/commercial packets, stale/cross-SHA rejection.
findings.fail: A script, fixture, old run, or unbound artifact is counted as current release proof.

findings.validation: Old/new producer-consumer matrix, unknown major, malformed payload, replay after upgrade, rolling deploy, deterministic upcast hash.
findings.fail: Stored/replayed work silently changes meaning across deploys.

findings.validation: Header spoof, session theft, role downgrade, disabled admin, expired support lease, readonly write, missing step-up, break-glass audit.
findings.fail: Shared Manager credentials or caller-selected headers impersonate platform users.

findings.validation: Separate payer/beneficiary, shared employee, free allowance, paid overage, plan change, suspension, refund, correction, invoice retry/reconciliation.
findings.fail: Entitlement, budget, provider cost, labor outcome, payer, and invoice attribution remain conflated.

findings.validation: Versioned entitlement allow/deny matrix for free, paid, suspended, overage, refund, and plan changes.
findings.fail: Paid limits default allow or cannot be audited to a policy version.

findings.validation: Role snapshots, multiple employees, revoked assignment, empty/degraded/ambiguous states, mobile review, moderated operator test.
findings.threshold: 0 unauthorized; operator identifies decision,cost,proof,repair without support
findings.fail: Users see wrong assignments or require internal IDs/infrastructure vocabulary.

findings.validation: Automated claim scan, missing/broken proof reference, evidence-tier comparison.
findings.fail: Public language exceeds the strongest current release-bound proof.

findings.validation: Kill -9, rolling restart, two workers, provider stall, DB outage, queue surge, alert delivery, repair replay.
findings.threshold: 0 lost; bounded duplicates; alerts within SLO; backlog drains
findings.fail: API health is treated as worker liveness or recovery loses work.

findings.validation: Seed stale SHA, migration number, standard status, proof IDs, and contradictory deployment state.
findings.fail: Canonical active docs report conflicting release state.

findings.validation: Estimator labeling/routing scan and proof-manifest contamination test.
findings.fail: The estimator is treated as product UX, pricing authority, or normal-employee proof.

findings.validation: Valid arbitrary schema plus invalid, recursive, oversized, secret-injected, unsafe-action, and role-projection cases; provider-backed work object.
findings.fail: Generic materialization widens action scope or leaks secrets.

findings.validation: Web/SMS/voice handoff, duplicate event, wrong caller, revoked number, partial transcript, consent/retention, approval escalation, live packet.
findings.fail: Voice behaves as a separate agent or bypasses channel-specific authority.

findings.validation: Secret/connector non-transfer, revoked/circular delegation, partial failure, conflicting outputs, billing split, controlled workforce packet.
findings.fail: Delegation collapses employee identities, assignments, custody, or final accountability.

findings.validation: Missing baseline, failed hard gate, stale evidence, cross-release reuse, changed metric, unsupported claim.
findings.fail: A hypothesis or feature is promoted without release-bound requirement, metric, threshold, evidence, confidence, owner, and decision.

findings.validation: Register HRR/compiler/generative-UI hypotheses with baseline, falsifier, threshold, evidence expiry, and claim gate.
findings.fail: Architecture or synthetic benchmarks are described as production/search/conversion validation.

findings.validation: Register labor displacement/value hypotheses; bind work outcomes, retained human authority, provider cost, payer/beneficiary, baseline, and market evidence requirements.
findings.fail: Long-term labor/value claims are inferred from product architecture or unreconciled usage.

plan_pass: Every finding is present exactly once as primary ownership; counts match 4/13/6/4/2; contracts and lane dependencies resolve; every item has validation, metric, threshold, and fail condition; no cycles in lane dependency graph; no hard gate is waivable.
plan_fail: Missing/duplicate finding, count mismatch, invalid severity, unknown lane/contract/dependency, lane dependency cycle, missing validation field, non-measurable threshold, or a hard gate represented as an aggregate score.
